items.title: OpenAI raises $110B on $730B pre-money valuation
items.raw_summary: The new funding consists of a $50 billion investment from Amazon as well as $30 billion each from Nvidia and SoftBank, against a $730 billion valuation. (HN: 207 pts, 330 comments)
items.why_it_matters: OpenAI raising $110B at a $730B valuation from Amazon, Nvidia, and SoftBank is the largest private funding round in history and reshapes the competitive landscape.
items.action: Understand the new capital dynamics in AI

items.why_it_matters: Dario Amodei's public statement on Anthropic's discussions with the Department of War signals a critical inflection point in AI companies' relationship with military applications.
items.action: Read Anthropic's full statement on defense policy

items.title: Did Apple just kill every iOS vibe coding app?
items.raw_summary:
Did Apple just kill every iOS vibe coding app?

Apple just dropped Xcode 26.3 with agentic coding.

Claude Code + Codex are now built into Xcode.

And with MCP support. https://t.co/lRPZabSMQ8
items.why_it_matters: Apple integrating Claude Code and Codex directly into Xcode 26.3 with MCP support could consolidate agentic coding into the native IDE for millions of iOS/macOS developers.
items.action: Update Xcode and test the new agentic features

items.title: 4% of GitHub public commits are being authored by Claude Code right now. At the current trajectory,
items.raw_summary: 4% of GitHub public commits are being authored by Claude Code right now. At the current trajectory, we believe that Claude Code will be 20%+ of all daily commits by the end of 2026. While you blinked, AI consumed all of software development. https://t.co/pFti4r6uR9
items.why_it_matters: SemiAnalysis reports 4% of public GitHub commits are now authored by Claude Code, projecting 20%+ by year-end — a concrete measure of AI's penetration into professional software development.
items.action: Assess how AI-authored code changes your review process

items.title: 📦 ollama/ollama v0.17.4
items.raw_summary: ## New models - [Qwen 3.5](https://ollama.com/library/qwen3.5): a family of open-source multimodal models that delivers exceptional utility and performance. - [LFM 2](https://ollama.com/library/lfm2): LFM2 is a family of hybrid models designed for on-device deployment. LFM2-24B-A
items.why_it_matters: Ollama v0.17.4 adds Qwen 3.5 and LFM 2 model families, expanding what developers can run locally without API costs.
items.action: Pull the new models with ollama pull qwen3.5

items.title: We've reset rate limits for all Claude Code users.
items.raw_summary:
We've reset rate limits for all Claude Code users.

Yesterday we rolled out a bug with prompt caching that caused usage limits to be consumed faster than normal. This is hotfixed in 2.1.62.

Make sure you upgrade to the latest and hope you enjoy using Claude Code this weekend!
items.why_it_matters: Anthropic reset all Claude Code rate limits after a prompt caching bug caused accelerated usage consumption — users should upgrade to v2.1.62 immediately.
items.action: Run claude update to get v2.1.62

items.title: Claude ❤️ Open Source
items.raw_summary:
Claude ❤️ Open Source 

We're giving 6 months of free Claude Max 20x to open source maintainers and core contributors. 

If you maintain a popular project or contribute across open source, please apply!
items.why_it_matters: Anthropic offering 6 months of free Claude Max 20x to open source maintainers is a significant investment in the OSS ecosystem and sets a precedent for AI companies supporting open source.
items.action: Apply if you maintain a popular open source project

items.raw_summary: OpenAI Certifications & OpenAI Jobs Platform: helping people learn how to use AI, and helping businesses find people who are skilled at using AI. https://t.co/glBBnHOy1O

items.title: Introducing the Evals API.
items.raw_summary:
Introducing the Evals API.

You can now programmatically define tests, automate evaluation runs, and quickly iterate on prompts.

Evals are still available in the dashboard—and now through the API, so you can integrate them anywhere in your workflow. https://t.co/OhHNd1wSGg
items.why_it_matters: The Evals API lets developers programmatically define tests and automate evaluation runs, making prompt iteration and quality assurance systematically integrable into CI/CD pipelines.
items.action: Integrate the Evals API into your prompt development workflow

items.title: We gave terabytes of CI logs to an LLM
items.raw_summary: We gave our AI agent a SQL interface to billions of CI log lines in ClickHouse. How we ingest, store, and query 1.5 billion log lines a week. (HN: 139 pts, 82 comments)
items.why_it_matters: Feeding terabytes of CI logs into an LLM via SQL/ClickHouse demonstrates a practical, battle-tested pattern for making massive operational data queryable by AI agents.
items.action: Consider a SQL interface for your own log pipelines

items.title: 🚨 Holy shit… Harvard and Stanford recently released the most unsettling AI agent paper I've read th
items.raw_summary:
🚨 Holy shit… Harvard and Stanford recently released the most unsettling AI agent paper I've read this year.

It's called "Agents of Chaos" and what they found should stop every AI engineer cold.

No theoretical simulations. No cherry-picked benchmarks. A live lab. Real infrastructure. Real failures.

Here's what emerged:

- Agents complied with non-owners who impersonated admins
- Sensitive information leaked across agent boundaries
- One agent executed destructive system-level commands
- Cross
items.why_it_matters: The Harvard/Stanford 'Agents of Chaos' paper found AI agents comply with impersonated admins and leak sensitive info in live infrastructure tests — a wake-up call for agent security.
items.action: Review agent authorization patterns in your deployments

items.title: New as of today: @stripe MCP in @OpenAI Responses API. Just plop in your API key and prompt Stripe i
items.raw_summary:
New as of today: @stripe MCP in @OpenAI Responses API. Just plop in your API key and prompt Stripe in your agentic workflows. 

Here’s a quick demo. (Feedback and feature requests welcome!) https://t.co/pA4e1vg563
items.why_it_matters: Stripe MCP integration in OpenAI's Responses API lets agents handle payments directly in agentic workflows, a key building block for autonomous commerce.
items.action: Test Stripe MCP in your agent workflows

items.title: 📦 openclaw/openclaw openclaw 2026.2.26
items.raw_summary: ### Changes  - Highlight: External Secrets Management introduces a full `openclaw secrets` workflow (`audit`, `configure`, `apply`, `reload`) with runtime snapshot activation, strict `secrets apply` target-path validation, safer migration scrubbing, ref-only auth-profile support
items.why_it_matters: OpenClaw 2026.2.26 introduces full external secrets management with audit/configure/apply/reload workflow, addressing a critical gap in agent security infrastructure.
items.action: Upgrade and run openclaw secrets audit

items.title: 🚨 Anthropic just open-sourced the exact Skills library their own engineers use internally.
items.raw_summary:
🚨 Anthropic just open-sourced the exact Skills library their own engineers use internally.

Stop building Claude workflows from scratch.

These are plug-and-play components that work across Claude Code, API, SDK, and VS Code copy once, deploy everywhere.

What's inside:

→ Excel + PowerPoint generation out of the box
→ File handling and document workflows
→ MCP-ready subagent building blocks
→ Pre-built patterns for multi-step automation
→ Production templates you'd normally spend weeks writing
items.why_it_matters: Anthropic open-sourcing their internal Skills library gives developers production-ready, plug-and-play components for Claude Code, API, and SDK workflows instead of building from scratch.
items.action: Browse and adopt relevant skills from the repo

items.title: A Chinese official’s use of ChatGPT revealed an intimidation operation
items.raw_summary: A sprawling Chinese influence operation — accidentally revealed by a Chinese law enforcement official’s use of ChatGPT — focused on intimidating Chinese dissidents abroad, including by impersonating US immigration officials, according to a new report from ChatGPT-maker OpenAI. (HN: 65 pts, 45 comments)
items.why_it_matters: A Chinese influence operation targeting dissidents abroad was accidentally exposed through a law enforcement official's ChatGPT usage, revealing real-world AI-enabled state surveillance.
items.action: Factor AI-enabled social engineering into your threat model

items.title: 📦 ggerganov/llama.cpp b8179
items.raw_summary: <details open>  CUDA: add CDNA3 MFMA support for flash attention MMA kernel (#19806)  * CUDA: add CDNA3 MFMA support for flash attention MMA kernel  Add MI300X (gfx942) MFMA tensor core flash attention using v_mfma_f32_16x16x16_f16 (FP16 in, FP32 accumulate).  - Add FATTN_WARP_SI

items.title: Even Sam Altman is admitting it now.
items.raw_summary:
Even Sam Altman is admitting it now.

Some companies are using "AI" as cover for plain old layoffs.

They didn't replace your job with an agent. They made bad bets, burned cash, and needed to cut headcount. Blaming AI just sounds better to investors than "we mismanaged the business."

The data backs this up. Nearly 90% of execs surveyed said AI had no impact on employment over the past 3 years. 

So why the narrative? 

Because "we're restructuring for AI" gets a stock bump. "We're cutting costs
items.why_it_matters: Sam Altman acknowledging companies use 'AI' as cover for layoffs, with data showing 90% of execs report no AI employment impact, challenges the dominant narrative about AI job displacement.
items.action: Scrutinize AI-layoff claims with actual data
